vitality.primary_driver_read: sleep, rhythm, metabolic load, and low fitness

optional_actions.label: Physical activity / fitness opportunity pool

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Guideline and cohort evidence strongly support direction, but C1 and C2 QALY magnitudes require causal haircut and utility translation.

overlap_rules: C2 capacity is credited once for the activity/fitness lever, never once per disease domain.
C1 disease-prevention slices are included inside the bundle anchor and should not be added again from legacy physical-activity slices.
C3 resilience is credited once as a general reserve effect, not separately per downstream disease.
Do not rank the bundle itself as a patient instruction when a more atomic action is present.

optional_actions.label: GLP-1/GIP metabolic therapy

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Weight-loss efficacy is RCT-backed; cardiovascular outcome value is high only in close-match established-CVD populations and model-translated in primary prevention.

overlap_rules: Do not double count weight-mediated VO2max improvement already credited to physical activity.
Pair with resistance training or nutrition guardrails when lean-mass loss would reduce capacity value.
Keep adherence and medication access as feasibility axes, not intrinsic QALY haircuts.

optional_actions.label: Supervised resistance training

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Resistance training has strong function evidence and supportive observational prevention evidence, but hard-outcome QALY magnitude is translated.

overlap_rules: If the broad physical_activity bundle is active, allocate shared capacity value rather than summing both totals.
When paired with GLP-1/GIP therapy, credit lean-mass preservation once.
Do not substitute generic activity minutes for progressive resistance work.

optional_actions.label: pioglitazone_stroke_mi_ir

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Hard-outcome randomized evidence supports recurrent stroke/MI reduction in a narrow IRIS-like population; absolute patient QALY is runner-derived from explicit trial baseline risk, internal event-value modeling, adverse-effect burden cadence, and secondary-prevention overlap constraints, so confidence remains medium-capped.

overlap_rules: Member of secondary_stroke_prevention, recurrent_mi_prevention, insulin_resistance, diabetes_prevention, medication_harm, heart_failure_harm, fracture_harm, hospitalization, disability, and mortality overlap families; do not sum with antiplatelet therapy, statins, BP control, smoking cessation, GLP-1/SGLT2, metformin, lifestyle, sleep apnea treatment, carotid intervention, or rehab records unless endpoint slices are explicitly partitioned.
HF, edema, weight, and fracture harms are handled as burden/adverse-effect vigilance here; if endpoint-specific harm event models are later added, reduce this burden slice to avoid double counting.

optional_actions.label: dpp_lifestyle

evidence_axis.basis_summary: DPP-style evidence supports incident diabetes reduction, but this fully derived record credits only the C1 diabetes-prevention channel; dose-specific C2/C3 lifestyle value remains uncredited until a lifestyle multichannel runner is available.

overlap_rules: Member of weight_loss_lifestyle overlap family; do not add independently to ir_lifestyle_prevention, direct_weight_remission, physical_activity_metabolic, or med_diet_metabolic unless endpoint and mechanism slices are partitioned.
Do not double count with incretin_prevention_obesity or metabolic_surgery; use one dominant weight-loss pathway or explicit incremental sequence model.
If physical activity or Mediterranean diet records are also selected, count shared coaching, diet, and exercise burden once and avoid duplicate C2 capacity credit.

optional_actions.label: metformin_prevention

evidence_axis.basis_summary: RCT evidence directly supports incident-diabetes prevention in DPP-like prediabetes, but patient QALY remains medium-capped because the record uses model-translated incident-diabetes event value, phenotype-bound baseline risk, adherence/tolerance assumptions, and internal medication-burden calibration.

overlap_rules: Member of diabetes_prevention overlap family; do not double count incident-diabetes prevention with dpp_lifestyle, incretin_prevention_obesity, ir_lifestyle_prevention, or other weight-loss/metabolic prevention records.
If physical activity, diet, weight loss, or incretin therapy is also selected, partition incident-diabetes prevention and shared behavior-support burden before scoring.
Do not credit downstream CKD, CVD, retinopathy, neuropathy, weight, energy, or monitoring-relief value unless mediated through a single incident-diabetes and complication-trajectory model.

optional_actions.label: act_zoster_vaccine_dementia

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Natural-experiment dementia association is causally guarded; established zoster and phn prevention are deliberately excluded pending endpoint-specific source upgrade, so the record is fully runner-computed but low-confidence and endpoint-limited.

overlap_rules: Do not double count the same dementia, cognition, function, falls, sensory, pain, or independence value with adjacent neuro, sensory, vaccine, lifestyle, mood, sleep, or multidomain records.
If the action is embedded in a broader preventive or cognitive program, shared coaching, scheduling, procedure, technology, and follow-up burden must be assigned once.
Any direct capacity benefit requires a separate sourced C2 utility anchor and explicit endpoint partition before it can be added.

optional_actions.label: physical_activity_metabolic

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Dose-response evidence supports activity for diabetes prevention, but this narrow valuation is medium-capped because absolute QALY is phenotype-bound, model-translated, burden-calibrated internally, and excludes shared activity C2 to avoid overlap.

overlap_rules: Do not double count incident-diabetes prevention with DPP lifestyle, metformin prevention, GLP-1/GIP weight loss, direct weight remission, Mediterranean diet, or other weight-loss records unless endpoint slices are partitioned.

Do not add general activity C2 capacity here when the physical_activity bundle or resistance/aerobic prescriptions already credit shared fitness capacity. Count activity time, coaching, soreness, and injury burden once.

optional_actions.label: acarbose_cv_igt

evidence_axis.basis_summary: STOP-NIDDM provides randomized relative-effect evidence for an IGT cardiovascular composite, but confidence remains low because absolute value is internally baseline-bound, model-translated, old-standard-care dependent, GI-burden sensitive, and endpoint-limited.

overlap_rules: Do not double count diabetes-prevention, weight-loss, BP, lipid, smoking, aspirin, GLP-1/GIP, SGLT2, metformin, lifestyle, or nutrition value unless endpoint slices are explicitly partitioned.
This promoted valuation credits only the cardiovascular composite event slice; diabetes-onset delay, hypertension prevention, and glycemic symptom effects remain excluded.
Shared medication review, glucose monitoring, GI adverse effects, adherence, and discontinuation burden must be assigned once across cardiometabolic medication records.

optional_actions.label: act_bp_control_dementia

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Source-backed hard-outcome action promoted into the shared QALY event runner; confidence is capped by model-translated value, default baseline-risk binding, provisional burden, and overlap handling.

overlap_rules: Do not double count incident probable dementia / MCI (adjudicated composite) against other actions claiming the same endpoint or causal pathway.
When a more specific procedure, diagnostic, genetic, or lifestyle runner exists for this phenotype, prefer that runner and treat this event row as the fallback or residual-risk row.
Suppress if eligibility, contraindication, prior completion, or patient preference makes the action non-executable.

optional_actions.label: act_statin_stroke_prevention

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Source-backed hard-outcome action promoted into the shared QALY event runner; confidence is capped by model-translated value, default baseline-risk binding, provisional burden, and overlap handling.

overlap_rules: Do not double count incident ischemic stroke per 1.0 mmol/L LDL reduction (cerebrovascular slice; coronary/MACE stock stays in CVD) against other actions claiming the same endpoint or causal pathway.
When a more specific procedure, diagnostic, genetic, or lifestyle runner exists for this phenotype, prefer that runner and treat this event row as the fallback or residual-risk row.
Suppress if eligibility, contraindication, prior completion, or patient preference makes the action non-executable.

optional_actions.label: physical_activity_rx

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Source-backed hard-outcome action promoted into the shared QALY event runner; confidence is capped by model-translated value, default baseline-risk binding, provisional burden, and overlap handling.

overlap_rules: Do not double count all-cause and cardiovascular mortality, incident type-2 diabetes, activity-sensitive cancer incidence (colorectal/postmenopausal breast/endometrial), and incident dementia -- the cross-cutting physical-activity volume lever. Keep cardiorespiratory fitness / VO2 max separate as a destination phenotype or mediator, not as the generic exercise-prescription object. against other actions claiming the same endpoint or causal pathway.
When a more specific procedure, diagnostic, genetic, or lifestyle runner exists for this phenotype, prefer that runner and treat this event row as the fallback or residual-risk row.
Suppress if eligibility, contraindication, prior completion, or patient preference makes the action non-executable.

optional_actions.label: med_diet_metabolic

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Source-backed hard-outcome action promoted into the shared QALY event runner; confidence is capped by model-translated value, default baseline-risk binding, provisional burden, and overlap handling.

overlap_rules: Do not double count incident type-2 diabetes (Mediterranean dietary-pattern lever, metabolic slice of the mediterranean_diet bundle) against other actions claiming the same endpoint or causal pathway.
When a more specific procedure, diagnostic, genetic, or lifestyle runner exists for this phenotype, prefer that runner and treat this event row as the fallback or residual-risk row.
Suppress if eligibility, contraindication, prior completion, or patient preference makes the action non-executable.

optional_actions.label: physical_activity_cancer

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Source-backed hard-outcome action promoted into the shared QALY event runner; confidence is capped by model-translated value, default baseline-risk binding, provisional burden, and overlap handling.

overlap_rules: Do not double count incident activity-sensitive cancers (colorectal/colon, postmenopausal breast, endometrial; basket also includes kidney, liver, NHL) against other actions claiming the same endpoint or causal pathway.
When a more specific procedure, diagnostic, genetic, or lifestyle runner exists for this phenotype, prefer that runner and treat this event row as the fallback or residual-risk row.
Suppress if eligibility, contraindication, prior completion, or patient preference makes the action non-executable.

optional_actions.label: act_physical_activity_dementia

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Source-backed hard-outcome action promoted into the shared QALY event runner; confidence is capped by model-translated value, default baseline-risk binding, provisional burden, and overlap handling.

overlap_rules: Do not double count incident all-cause dementia (and Alzheimer's disease) via the leisure-time physical-activity volume lever against other actions claiming the same endpoint or causal pathway.
When a more specific procedure, diagnostic, genetic, or lifestyle runner exists for this phenotype, prefer that runner and treat this event row as the fallback or residual-risk row.
Suppress if eligibility, contraindication, prior completion, or patient preference makes the action non-executable.

optional_actions.label: ir_lifestyle_prevention

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Source-backed hard-outcome action promoted into the shared QALY event runner; confidence is capped by model-translated value, default baseline-risk binding, provisional burden, and overlap handling.

overlap_rules: Do not double count incident T2DM (IR phenotype caught before HbA1c crosses the prediabetes threshold) against other actions claiming the same endpoint or causal pathway.
When a more specific procedure, diagnostic, genetic, or lifestyle runner exists for this phenotype, prefer that runner and treat this event row as the fallback or residual-risk row.
Suppress if eligibility, contraindication, prior completion, or patient preference makes the action non-executable.

optional_actions.label: med_diet_cancer

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Source-backed hard-outcome action promoted into the shared QALY event runner; confidence is capped by model-translated value, default baseline-risk binding, provisional burden, and overlap handling.

overlap_rules: Do not double count incident diet-sensitive cancers (colorectal, breast, and the overall diet-sensitive basket) via a higher-quality Mediterranean dietary pattern against other actions claiming the same endpoint or causal pathway.
When a more specific procedure, diagnostic, genetic, or lifestyle runner exists for this phenotype, prefer that runner and treat this event row as the fallback or residual-risk row.
Suppress if eligibility, contraindication, prior completion, or patient preference makes the action non-executable.

diagnostics.label: Coronary calcium scoring

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Guideline-supported reclassification for uncertain statin decisions; downstream QALY is modeled from lipid/BP intensity changes.

diagnostics.dominant_reclassification_path: Plaque burden changes lipid/BP intensity, urgency, and sequencing.

diagnostics.label: Home sleep apnea test

evidence_axis.basis_summary: AASM-supported testing in high-pretest OSA phenotypes; value comes from symptom/BP sequencing while CV event prevention is explicitly excluded.

value_channel.basis: D7 mandatory label (trace F6). The 0.45 is defensible as felt-quality (symptom/energy) plus BP-treatment sequencing. It is explicitly NOT cardiovascular-prevention value: CPAP's CV-event evidence is null (SAVE). The same number read as CV prevention would be indefensible; the label is the guardrail on display.

diagnostics.dominant_reclassification_path: Sleep-disordered breathing confirmed, changing BP sequencing, fatigue/vitality interpretation, and treatment priority.

diagnostics.label: Home or ambulatory BP confirmation

evidence_axis.basis_summary: USPSTF-backed out-of-office confirmation with strong downstream treatment evidence and clear management threshold.

diagnostics.dominant_reclassification_path: Home or ambulatory BP average confirms persistent hypertension versus white-coat or measurement artifact, changing medication threshold and upstream driver sequencing.

diagnostics.label: ECG / Holter bradycardia workup

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Ambulatory ECG and QT safety monitoring are guideline/consensus-supported when phenotype or medication risk fits, but generic yield and QALY-if are context-heavy.

diagnostics.dominant_reclassification_path: Clinically meaningful bradyarrhythmia or conduction disease changes exercise clearance, sleep interpretation, cardiology escalation, and safety monitoring.

diagnostics.label: Oral glucose tolerance test

evidence_axis.basis_summary: ADA criteria make OGTT validity strong; yield and downstream value depend on ambiguous-risk phenotype and subsequent treatment path.

diagnostics.dominant_reclassification_path: Post-load diabetes confirmation changes diabetes labeling, medication eligibility, risk thresholds, and auditability of downstream QALY.

diagnostics.label: FIB-4 then FibroScan if indicated

evidence_axis.basis_summary: AASLD staged fibrosis assessment is guideline-supported in risk-qualified MASLD; QALY-if depends on stage and management actually changed.

diagnostics.dominant_reclassification_path: Fibrosis risk changes urgency of weight-loss therapy, alcohol guidance, metabolic medication selection, hepatology referral, and monitoring.

required_items.label: Colorectal cancer screening
required_items.reason: Age 47 and colorectal screening not current.

required_items.label: ATM genetic counseling and documentation
required_items.reason: ATM P/LP heterozygous finding requires documented guidance.

required_items.label: ATM family-history gate
required_items.reason: CONSIDER pancreatic surveillance (MRI/MRCP and/or EUS) only if a first- or second-degree relative has had pancreatic cancer

required_findings.interpretation: Use when primary-prevention lipid treatment is indicated by risk, risk enhancer, or shared decision threshold and contraindications are absent.
Use only after ferritin, iron indices, anemia status, and symptom context support iron deficiency as an active driver.
Use only when repeat TSH/free T4 and clinical context support treatment or specialist-directed follow-up.

excluded_items.label: Vitamin D repletion for confirmed deficiency

required_findings.interpretation: Use for confirmed deficiency, especially when bone health, falls risk, or muscle symptoms change the action sequence.

excluded_items.label: BP pharmacotherapy for confirmed stage-2 hypertension

required_findings.interpretation: Use after office, home, or ambulatory readings support persistent stage-2 hypertension and secondary causes or sequencing issues are considered.

Applies when proteinuric non-diabetic CKD persists despite appropriate background therapy and finerenone is clinically reasonable; confirm eGFR, albuminuria, potassium, BP, background RAAS/SGLT2 use, CKD etiology, contraindications, and monitoring capacity before recommendation.

Applies to an IMPROVE-IT-like post-acute-coronary-syndrome or very-high-risk ASCVD phenotype with residual LDL-C or apoB risk after maximally tolerated statin therapy; verify current LDL-C/apoB, statin intensity and adherence, recent ACS versus stable ASCVD context, additive PCSK9 or bempedoic options, drug tolerance, access, and overlap before recommendation.

Applies to a CLEAR-Outcomes-like high-risk primary- or secondary-prevention ASCVD phenotype with true or practical statin intolerance and residual LDL-C or apoB risk; verify statin intolerance/rechallenge history, baseline risk, LDL-C/apoB, gout or uric-acid risk, tendon risk, background ezetimibe/PCSK9 use, access, and overlap before recommendation.

Applies only to a SUMMIT-like obesity-related HFpEF phenotype where incretin therapy such as tirzepatide is clinically plausible; verify HFpEF diagnosis, EF, NYHA class, BMI, congestion status, prior HF events, diabetes, kidney function, frailty/sarcopenia risk, background HF/diuretic therapy, GI tolerance, access, and contraindications before recommendation.

Applies only to an overweight or insulin-resistant type-2 diabetes phenotype where metformin is incremental rather than already baseline care; verify diabetes diagnosis, BMI, A1c, ASCVD risk, kidney function, liver disease/alcohol risk, heart-failure status, B12 status, GI tolerance, pregnancy context, and current GLP-1/SGLT2/insulin or lifestyle counterfactual before recommendation.

Applies when the matched clinical finding is present and the patient meets the trial/guideline phenotype; contraindications must be checked before recommendation.

Applies to type 2 diabetes with albuminuria or renal-risk phenotype matching Steno-2 style intensive multifactorial care; do not score as additive to its component BP, lipid, glycemic, RAASi, antiplatelet, or lifestyle actions.
Applies to recent MI or high-risk coronary artery disease during the annual influenza vaccination window; contraindications and vaccination history must be checked before recommendation.

Applies only when AF and HFrEF phenotype matches CASTLE-AF style procedural candidacy; electrophysiology eligibility, rhythm strategy, competing device indications, and patient preference must be checked.
Applies to ischemic systolic cardiomyopathy primary-prevention ICD phenotype after guideline-directed medical therapy, EF/timing confirmation, life-expectancy gate, and device candidacy review.

Applies when elevated blood pressure creates actionable cardiovascular risk and medication intensification is clinically appropriate; confirm persistent BP elevation, orthostasis/fall risk, kidney function, potassium, pregnancy status, contraindications, and current treatment stack before recommendation.

Applies to high-risk or secondary-prevention ASCVD patients with residual LDL-C or apoB risk despite maximally tolerated statin and ezetimibe, or appropriate intolerance; verify LDL-C/apoB, event history, familial hypercholesterolemia, adherence, access, and injection acceptability.

Applies to a statin-treated REDUCE-IT-like phenotype with elevated triglycerides and established CVD or diabetes plus a risk factor; verify triglyceride range, LDL-C control, atrial-fibrillation history, bleeding risk, fish allergy/intolerance, antithrombotics, and access.

Applies to symptomatic HFrEF with LVEF at or below guideline threshold and wide QRS, especially LBBB, despite optimized medical therapy; verify NYHA class, QRS duration/morphology, rhythm or pacing indication, life expectancy, infection risk, device preference, and ICD candidacy.

Applies to recently symptomatic severe carotid stenosis with suitable anatomy; verify NASCET percent stenosis, symptom timing, surgical risk, age/sex subgroup, life expectancy, antiplatelet/statin/BP therapy, and local perioperative stroke or death rates.

Applies when obesity or overweight with high diabetes-risk phenotype matches incretin-trial eligibility and contraindications, pregnancy plans, pancreatobiliary history, access, and continuation plan have been checked.

Applies only when severe obesity or obesity with qualifying comorbidity meets surgical eligibility and shared-decision thresholds; operative risk, contraindications, anatomy, prior surgery, and patient preference must be checked.

Narrow map-eligible valuation for active tobacco use in an albuminuric CKD phenotype where structured cessation is expected to reduce CKD progression risk. This does not claim the full tobacco-cessation opportunity across cancer, COPD, or cardiovascular endpoints.

Narrow map-eligible valuation for hazardous or heavy alcohol use where the patient matches a recurrent atrial-fibrillation risk phenotype and reduction or abstinence is clinically safe. It does not claim full liver, cancer, trauma, mood, sleep, or BP-mediated alcohol value.

Narrow map-eligible valuation for a SSaSS-like salt-substitution or sodium-potassium exposure intervention in elevated-BP or high-sodium phenotype. It does not claim the full diet, weight, exercise, CKD, or dementia lifestyle bundle value.

Applies only to a confirmed or strongly suspected idiopathic normal-pressure hydrocephalus phenotype with gait-predominant impairment, ventriculomegaly, mimics excluded, acceptable surgical risk, and response to temporary CSF drainage or equivalent shunt-candidacy evidence. This valuation is not for nonspecific gait decline.

Applies only when vision-impairing cataract is present and cataract extraction is clinically appropriate; visual acuity/contrast limitation, functional impairment, ocular comorbidity, first-eye or second-eye status, cognitive baseline, and surgical risk must be checked.

Applies only to an older adult with elevated cognitive-risk phenotype, preserved ability to participate, and a feasible speed-of-processing training protocol. Baseline cognition, sensory barriers, digital access, depression/sleep confounders, completed sessions, boosters, and multidomain-program overlap must be checked.

Applies only to chronic coronary disease or recent MI patients with residual inflammatory risk where low-dose colchicine is clinically appropriate after standard lipid, BP, antithrombotic, smoking, and diabetes risk management are addressed. Baseline risk is bound to the CAD residual-inflammation trial phenotype, not to a general elevated-hs-CRP population.

Applies only when pregnancy is present and gestational diabetes is screen-detected or otherwise confirmed, with treatment to pregnancy glycemic targets clinically appropriate; baseline risk is bound to a treated-eligible GDM pregnancy phenotype rather than all pregnancies.

Applies only for adults with obesity and elevated diabetes risk, especially impaired glucose tolerance or prediabetes, where orlistat plus lifestyle support is clinically appropriate and competing anti-obesity therapy is not already claiming the same diabetes-prevention value.

Applies only for postmenopausal women with elevated validated breast-cancer risk, a meaningful ER-positive preventable fraction, acceptable bone/fracture risk, and willingness to consider aromatase-inhibitor chemoprevention.

Applies only for women with elevated validated breast-cancer risk who are clinically eligible for SERM chemoprevention after menopausal status, VTE/stroke risk, uterus/endometrial context, vasomotor symptoms, and patient preference are reviewed. Do not use for average-risk patients or when aromatase-inhibitor chemoprevention, BRCA surgery/surveillance, or screening VOI supersedes the decision.

Applies only when CKD progression risk is materially BP-driven, baseline BP remains above the individualized intensive target, and orthostasis, frailty, AKI, potassium, and medication-stack risks make intensification clinically reasonable.

Applies when reduced eGFR, AKI risk, or a transition of care creates a real opportunity to review active renally cleared or nephrotoxic medications and make an accepted medication change. Do not score when medication data are unavailable or no actionable exposure exists.

Narrow map-eligible valuation for a recent-onset, non-insulin type 2 diabetes phenotype where structured intentional weight loss can plausibly induce remission. Requires diabetes duration, medication status, BMI, HbA1c, contraindications, and eating-disorder/frailty safety checks before recommendation.

Narrow low-confidence valuation for low-fiber or low-whole-grain intake where a sustained +8 g/day fiber-equivalent change is realistic and GI tolerance is acceptable. Requires baseline intake, food access, diet-quality context, and whether the change is part of a broader diet bundle before recommendation.

Applies narrowly to a confirmed pathogenic or likely pathogenic BRCA1/BRCA2 carrier with breast tissue present who is considering bilateral risk-reducing mastectomy after genetics and surgical counseling. Do not use for VUS-only findings, unconfirmed family history, prior bilateral mastectomy, active breast cancer treatment, or patients who would not consider surgery.

Applies narrowly to a confirmed Lynch syndrome mismatch-repair pathogenic or likely pathogenic variant carrier with uterus and/or ovaries present who is considering risk-reducing hysterectomy with bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy or a staged gynecologic risk-reduction plan after genetics and gynecologic oncology counseling. Do not use for VUS-only findings, completed relevant surgery, active gynecologic cancer treatment, unresolved fertility plans, or patients who would not consider surgery.

Narrow Batch 020 valuation for advancing, progressive, or high-risk CKD where early nephrology referral and multidisciplinary care are still actionable compared with late referral or unmanaged primary-care-only follow-up. Requires eGFR, slope, albuminuria, CKD cause, current management quality, existing nephrology access, transplant/dialysis planning relevance, and local access constraints before recommendation.

Narrow Batch 020 valuation for confirmed severe symptomatic aortic stenosis where valve replacement is clinically indicated and the counterfactual is no valve replacement or delayed/inaccessible valve intervention. Requires valve metrics, symptom status, EF and flow-gradient phenotype, frailty, STS/procedural risk, TAVR/SAVR candidacy, anatomy, life expectancy, patient goals, and competing noncardiac mortality before recommendation.

Established ASCVD secondary prevention antiplatelet candidate: prior MI, ischemic stroke/TIA, coronary revascularization, chronic coronary disease, symptomatic PAD, or documented ASCVD; exclude active major bleeding, aspirin allergy, concurrent anticoagulation unless specifically modeled, and active DAPT/other antithrombotic bundles that supersede aspirin monotherapy.

Use only for confirmed serial eGFR slope decline >5 mL/min/1.73m2/year, not a single low eGFR. Best fit is DAPA-CKD-like albuminuric CKD (eGFR 25-75 mL/min/1.73m2, UACR 200-5000 mg/g) on ACEi/ARB unless intolerant; exclude type 1 diabetes and contraindications.

excluded_items.label: CBC, iron studies, and thyroid panel
Anal HSIL detection and treatment pathway

warnings: Non-CVD risk domains remain marked pending live model wiring in source prototype.

clinical_plan.title: Ethan Park clinical plan from scored engine state
clinical_plan.summary: Ethan Park has 3 required items separated from the optional action map. The recommended next-step set is limited to 3 scored items after library scoring, diagnostic scoring, genetics routing, and AI candidate admission.

required_items.label: Colorectal cancer screening
required_items.reason: Age 47 and colorectal screening not current.

required_items.label: ATM genetic counseling and documentation
required_items.reason: ATM P/LP heterozygous finding requires documented guidance.

required_items.label: ATM family-history gate
required_items.reason: CONSIDER pancreatic surveillance (MRI/MRCP and/or EUS) only if a first- or second-degree relative has had pancreatic cancer

non_required_next_steps.label: GLP-1/GIP metabolic therapy

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Weight-loss efficacy is RCT-backed; cardiovascular outcome value is high only in close-match established-CVD populations and model-translated in primary prevention.

non_required_next_steps.why_now: Highest patient-value optional action after eligibility and required-item separation.

non_required_next_steps.label: Supervised resistance training

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Resistance training has strong function evidence and supportive observational prevention evidence, but hard-outcome QALY magnitude is translated.

non_required_next_steps.why_now: Highest patient-value optional action after eligibility and required-item separation.

non_required_next_steps.label: Home or ambulatory BP confirmation

evidence_axis.basis_summary: USPSTF-backed out-of-office confirmation with strong downstream treatment evidence and clear management threshold.

non_required_next_steps.why_now: Home or ambulatory BP average confirms persistent hypertension versus white-coat or measurement artifact, changing medication threshold and upstream driver sequencing.

vitality.primary_driver_read: iron, thyroid, sleep fragmentation, bone context, and low fitness

optional_actions.label: Physical activity / fitness opportunity pool

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Guideline and cohort evidence strongly support direction, but C1 and C2 QALY magnitudes require causal haircut and utility translation.

overlap_rules: C2 capacity is credited once for the activity/fitness lever, never once per disease domain.
C1 disease-prevention slices are included inside the bundle anchor and should not be added again from legacy physical-activity slices.
C3 resilience is credited once as a general reserve effect, not separately per downstream disease.
Do not rank the bundle itself as a patient instruction when a more atomic action is present.

optional_actions.label: Statin primary prevention

evidence_axis.basis_summary: RCT meta-analysis support for lipid lowering with patient-risk scaling; burden and exact patient QALY remain model-translated but direction is strong when indicated.

overlap_rules: Do not add a C2 healthspan benefit unless a separate patient-specific pathway is established.
Do not truncate fatal-event benefit solely because a cited endpoint is nonfatal.

optional_actions.label: Supervised resistance training

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Resistance training has strong function evidence and supportive observational prevention evidence, but hard-outcome QALY magnitude is translated.

overlap_rules: If the broad physical_activity bundle is active, allocate shared capacity value rather than summing both totals.
When paired with GLP-1/GIP therapy, credit lean-mass preservation once.
Do not substitute generic activity minutes for progressive resistance work.

optional_actions.label: fh_high_intensity_llt

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Source-backed hard-outcome action promoted into the shared QALY event runner; confidence is capped by model-translated value, default baseline-risk binding, provisional burden, and overlap handling.

overlap_rules: Do not double count composite coronary artery disease events + all-cause mortality in heterozygous familial hypercholesterolemia (moderate- to high-intensity statin / lipid-lowering therapy, primary prevention) against other actions claiming the same endpoint or causal pathway.
When a more specific procedure, diagnostic, genetic, or lifestyle runner exists for this phenotype, prefer that runner and treat this event row as the fallback or residual-risk row.
Suppress if eligibility, contraindication, prior completion, or patient preference makes the action non-executable.

optional_actions.label: fh_cascade_screening

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Source-backed hard-outcome action promoted into the shared QALY event runner; confidence is capped by model-translated value, default baseline-risk binding, provisional burden, and overlap handling.

overlap_rules: Do not double count major adverse cardiovascular events (ASCVD death / acute coronary syndrome, i.e. premature CAD events) in first-degree relatives identified and treated through FH cascade screening against other actions claiming the same endpoint or causal pathway.
When a more specific procedure, diagnostic, genetic, or lifestyle runner exists for this phenotype, prefer that runner and treat this event row as the fallback or residual-risk row.
Suppress if eligibility, contraindication, prior completion, or patient preference makes the action non-executable.

optional_actions.label: lpa_reclassify_intensify

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Source-backed hard-outcome action promoted into the shared QALY event runner; confidence is capped by model-translated value, default baseline-risk binding, provisional burden, and overlap handling.

overlap_rules: Do not double count major vascular events (MI, stroke, coronary revascularization) in patients reclassified to higher ASCVD risk by elevated Lp(a), via earlier/more-intensive LDL-lowering (and BP) control against other actions claiming the same endpoint or causal pathway.
When a more specific procedure, diagnostic, genetic, or lifestyle runner exists for this phenotype, prefer that runner and treat this event row as the fallback or residual-risk row.
Suppress if eligibility, contraindication, prior completion, or patient preference makes the action non-executable.

optional_actions.label: physical_activity_rx

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Source-backed hard-outcome action promoted into the shared QALY event runner; confidence is capped by model-translated value, default baseline-risk binding, provisional burden, and overlap handling.

overlap_rules: Do not double count all-cause and cardiovascular mortality, incident type-2 diabetes, activity-sensitive cancer incidence (colorectal/postmenopausal breast/endometrial), and incident dementia -- the cross-cutting physical-activity volume lever. Keep cardiorespiratory fitness / VO2 max separate as a destination phenotype or mediator, not as the generic exercise-prescription object. against other actions claiming the same endpoint or causal pathway.
When a more specific procedure, diagnostic, genetic, or lifestyle runner exists for this phenotype, prefer that runner and treat this event row as the fallback or residual-risk row.
Suppress if eligibility, contraindication, prior completion, or patient preference makes the action non-executable.

diagnostics.label: Coronary calcium scoring

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Guideline-supported reclassification for uncertain statin decisions; downstream QALY is modeled from lipid/BP intensity changes.

diagnostics.dominant_reclassification_path: Plaque burden changes lipid/BP intensity, urgency, and sequencing.

diagnostics.label: CBC, iron studies, and thyroid panel

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Fatigue lab yield is evidence-bounded and low; downstream benefit is real only when true iron deficiency or overt thyroid dysfunction is found.

diagnostics.dominant_reclassification_path: Iron deficiency, anemia, clinically significant thyroid dysfunction, or a combined reversible fatigue driver changes treatment and training sequence.

diagnostics.label: Vitamin D recheck

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Broad asymptomatic vitamin D screening is not guideline-supported and broad fracture-prevention benefit is not shown.

diagnostics.dominant_reclassification_path: Confirmed deficiency changes supplement dosing, bone-health sequencing, and resistance-training guardrails.

diagnostics.label: Baseline DEXA

evidence_axis.basis_summary: DXA bone screening is guideline-supported in eligible women/risk groups; lean-mass/body-composition interpretation is weaker and should be split later.

diagnostics.dominant_reclassification_path: Low bone density, high visceral fat, or low lean mass changes resistance training, nutrition, medication thresholds, and monitoring cadence.

diagnostics.label: Insomnia / sleep regularity screen

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Actigraphy is low-burden and conditionally guideline-supported, but generic sleep-fragmentation reclassification and QALY value are weak compared with phenotype-driven OSA testing.

diagnostics.dominant_reclassification_path: Insomnia, fragmented sleep, menopause symptoms, mood, or schedule irregularity changes recovery intervention and training sequence.

required_findings.interpretation: Use only after ferritin, iron indices, anemia status, and symptom context support iron deficiency as an active driver.
Use only when repeat TSH/free T4 and clinical context support treatment or specialist-directed follow-up.

excluded_items.label: Vitamin D repletion for confirmed deficiency

required_findings.interpretation: Use for confirmed deficiency, especially when bone health, falls risk, or muscle symptoms change the action sequence.
Use when BMI and comorbidity criteria are met and contraindications, access, side effects, lean-mass protection, and durability are addressed.

excluded_items.label: BP pharmacotherapy for confirmed stage-2 hypertension

required_findings.interpretation: Use after office, home, or ambulatory readings support persistent stage-2 hypertension and secondary causes or sequencing issues are considered.
Required preventive care gap. Route as must-do even when ranking and patient-QALY display are separate.
Required genetics guidance and condition-specific surveillance routing. Specific actions depend on sex, family history, variant context, and current guidelines.

Applies when the matched clinical finding is present and the patient meets the trial/guideline phenotype; contraindications must be checked before recommendation.
Applies when the matched clinical finding is present and the patient meets the trial/guideline phenotype; contraindications must be checked before recommendation.
Applies when the matched clinical finding is present and the patient meets the trial/guideline phenotype; contraindications must be checked before recommendation.
Applies when the matched clinical finding is present and the patient meets the trial/guideline phenotype; contraindications must be checked before recommendation.

Applies when proteinuric non-diabetic CKD persists despite appropriate background therapy and finerenone is clinically reasonable; confirm eGFR, albuminuria, potassium, BP, background RAAS/SGLT2 use, CKD etiology, contraindications, and monitoring capacity before recommendation.
Applies when the matched clinical finding is present and the patient meets the trial/guideline phenotype; contraindications must be checked before recommendation.
Applies when the matched clinical finding is present and the patient meets the trial/guideline phenotype; contraindications must be checked before recommendation.
Applies when the matched clinical finding is present and the patient meets the trial/guideline phenotype; contraindications must be checked before recommendation.

Applies to an IMPROVE-IT-like post-acute-coronary-syndrome or very-high-risk ASCVD phenotype with residual LDL-C or apoB risk after maximally tolerated statin therapy; verify current LDL-C/apoB, statin intensity and adherence, recent ACS versus stable ASCVD context, additive PCSK9 or bempedoic options, drug tolerance, access, and overlap before recommendation.

Applies to a CLEAR-Outcomes-like high-risk primary- or secondary-prevention ASCVD phenotype with true or practical statin intolerance and residual LDL-C or apoB risk; verify statin intolerance/rechallenge history, baseline risk, LDL-C/apoB, gout or uric-acid risk, tendon risk, background ezetimibe/PCSK9 use, access, and overlap before recommendation.

Applies only to a SUMMIT-like obesity-related HFpEF phenotype where incretin therapy such as tirzepatide is clinically plausible; verify HFpEF diagnosis, EF, NYHA class, BMI, congestion status, prior HF events, diabetes, kidney function, frailty/sarcopenia risk, background HF/diuretic therapy, GI tolerance, access, and contraindications before recommendation.

Applies only to an overweight or insulin-resistant type-2 diabetes phenotype where metformin is incremental rather than already baseline care; verify diabetes diagnosis, BMI, A1c, ASCVD risk, kidney function, liver disease/alcohol risk, heart-failure status, B12 status, GI tolerance, pregnancy context, and current GLP-1/SGLT2/insulin or lifestyle counterfactual before recommendation.

Applies only to an IRIS-like non-diabetic insulin-resistant patient after recent ischemic stroke or TIA where pioglitazone is clinically plausible for secondary vascular prevention; verify stroke/TIA timing and subtype, insulin-resistance definition, diabetes exclusion, recurrent-event risk, heart-failure/edema risk, weight and fracture risk, bladder/liver history, background antiplatelet/statin/BP therapy, sleep apnea/smoking/lifestyle care, and contraindications before recommendation.
Applies when the matched clinical finding is present and the patient meets the trial/guideline phenotype; contraindications must be checked before recommendation.
Applies when the matched clinical finding is present and the patient meets the trial/guideline phenotype; contraindications must be checked before recommendation.
Applies when the matched clinical finding is present and the patient meets the trial/guideline phenotype; contraindications must be checked before recommendation.
Applies when the matched clinical finding is present and the patient meets the trial/guideline phenotype; contraindications must be checked before recommendation.
Applies when the matched clinical finding is present and the patient meets the trial/guideline phenotype; contraindications must be checked before recommendation.
Applies when the matched clinical finding is present and the patient meets the trial/guideline phenotype; contraindications must be checked before recommendation.
Applies when the matched clinical finding is present and the patient meets the trial/guideline phenotype; contraindications must be checked before recommendation.

Applies to type 2 diabetes with albuminuria or renal-risk phenotype matching Steno-2 style intensive multifactorial care; do not score as additive to its component BP, lipid, glycemic, RAASi, antiplatelet, or lifestyle actions.
Applies to recent MI or high-risk coronary artery disease during the annual influenza vaccination window; contraindications and vaccination history must be checked before recommendation.

Applies only when AF and HFrEF phenotype matches CASTLE-AF style procedural candidacy; electrophysiology eligibility, rhythm strategy, competing device indications, and patient preference must be checked.
Applies to ischemic systolic cardiomyopathy primary-prevention ICD phenotype after guideline-directed medical therapy, EF/timing confirmation, life-expectancy gate, and device candidacy review.

Applies when elevated blood pressure creates actionable cardiovascular risk and medication intensification is clinically appropriate; confirm persistent BP elevation, orthostasis/fall risk, kidney function, potassium, pregnancy status, contraindications, and current treatment stack before recommendation.

Applies to high-risk or secondary-prevention ASCVD patients with residual LDL-C or apoB risk despite maximally tolerated statin and ezetimibe, or appropriate intolerance; verify LDL-C/apoB, event history, familial hypercholesterolemia, adherence, access, and injection acceptability.

Applies to a statin-treated REDUCE-IT-like phenotype with elevated triglycerides and established CVD or diabetes plus a risk factor; verify triglyceride range, LDL-C control, atrial-fibrillation history, bleeding risk, fish allergy/intolerance, antithrombotics, and access.

Applies to symptomatic HFrEF with LVEF at or below guideline threshold and wide QRS, especially LBBB, despite optimized medical therapy; verify NYHA class, QRS duration/morphology, rhythm or pacing indication, life expectancy, infection risk, device preference, and ICD candidacy.

Applies to recently symptomatic severe carotid stenosis with suitable anatomy; verify NASCET percent stenosis, symptom timing, surgical risk, age/sex subgroup, life expectancy, antiplatelet/statin/BP therapy, and local perioperative stroke or death rates.

Applies when obesity or overweight with high diabetes-risk phenotype matches incretin-trial eligibility and contraindications, pregnancy plans, pancreatobiliary history, access, and continuation plan have been checked.

Applies only when severe obesity or obesity with qualifying comorbidity meets surgical eligibility and shared-decision thresholds; operative risk, contraindications, anatomy, prior surgery, and patient preference must be checked.

Applies when prediabetes or high diabetes-risk phenotype is present and an intensive DPP-like weight-loss lifestyle intervention is plausible; baseline BMI, beta-cell reserve, achievable weight loss, and competing weight-loss plans must be checked.

Narrow map-eligible valuation for active tobacco use in an albuminuric CKD phenotype where structured cessation is expected to reduce CKD progression risk. This does not claim the full tobacco-cessation opportunity across cancer, COPD, or cardiovascular endpoints.

Narrow map-eligible valuation for hazardous or heavy alcohol use where the patient matches a recurrent atrial-fibrillation risk phenotype and reduction or abstinence is clinically safe. It does not claim full liver, cancer, trauma, mood, sleep, or BP-mediated alcohol value.

Narrow map-eligible valuation for a SSaSS-like salt-substitution or sodium-potassium exposure intervention in elevated-BP or high-sodium phenotype. It does not claim the full diet, weight, exercise, CKD, or dementia lifestyle bundle value.

Narrow map-eligible valuation for metformin prevention only when a DPP-like prediabetes/high-transition-risk phenotype is present and metformin is clinically appropriate after kidney function, B12 status, pregnancy context, GI tolerance, and competing lifestyle or incretin prevention are checked.

Applies only to a confirmed or strongly suspected idiopathic normal-pressure hydrocephalus phenotype with gait-predominant impairment, ventriculomegaly, mimics excluded, acceptable surgical risk, and response to temporary CSF drainage or equivalent shunt-candidacy evidence. This valuation is not for nonspecific gait decline.

Applies only when vision-impairing cataract is present and cataract extraction is clinically appropriate; visual acuity/contrast limitation, functional impairment, ocular comorbidity, first-eye or second-eye status, cognitive baseline, and surgical risk must be checked.

Applies only when age, immune status, vaccine history, contraindications, and preventive-care context make recombinant zoster vaccination clinically appropriate. Dementia-risk phenotype stratifies the valuation but does not replace routine vaccine eligibility.

Applies only to an older adult with elevated cognitive-risk phenotype, preserved ability to participate, and a feasible speed-of-processing training protocol. Baseline cognition, sensory barriers, digital access, depression/sleep confounders, completed sessions, boosters, and multidomain-program overlap must be checked.

Applies only to chronic coronary disease or recent MI patients with residual inflammatory risk where low-dose colchicine is clinically appropriate after standard lipid, BP, antithrombotic, smoking, and diabetes risk management are addressed. Baseline risk is bound to the CAD residual-inflammation trial phenotype, not to a general elevated-hs-CRP population.

Applies only when pregnancy is present and gestational diabetes is screen-detected or otherwise confirmed, with treatment to pregnancy glycemic targets clinically appropriate; baseline risk is bound to a treated-eligible GDM pregnancy phenotype rather than all pregnancies.

Applies only for adults with obesity and elevated diabetes risk, especially impaired glucose tolerance or prediabetes, where orlistat plus lifestyle support is clinically appropriate and competing anti-obesity therapy is not already claiming the same diabetes-prevention value.

Applies only for postmenopausal women with elevated validated breast-cancer risk, a meaningful ER-positive preventable fraction, acceptable bone/fracture risk, and willingness to consider aromatase-inhibitor chemoprevention.

Applies only for women with elevated validated breast-cancer risk who are clinically eligible for SERM chemoprevention after menopausal status, VTE/stroke risk, uterus/endometrial context, vasomotor symptoms, and patient preference are reviewed. Do not use for average-risk patients or when aromatase-inhibitor chemoprevention, BRCA surgery/surveillance, or screening VOI supersedes the decision.

Applies only when CKD progression risk is materially BP-driven, baseline BP remains above the individualized intensive target, and orthostasis, frailty, AKI, potassium, and medication-stack risks make intensification clinically reasonable.

Applies when reduced eGFR, AKI risk, or a transition of care creates a real opportunity to review active renally cleared or nephrotoxic medications and make an accepted medication change. Do not score when medication data are unavailable or no actionable exposure exists.

Narrow map-eligible valuation for a recent-onset, non-insulin type 2 diabetes phenotype where structured intentional weight loss can plausibly induce remission. Requires diabetes duration, medication status, BMI, HbA1c, contraindications, and eating-disorder/frailty safety checks before recommendation.

Narrow map-eligible valuation for a sedentary or low-activity prediabetes phenotype where a sustained leisure-time activity increase is feasible. Requires baseline activity, achieved MET-hours per week, mobility/cardiac safety, injury risk, and overlap with any broader physical-activity plan before recommendation.

Narrow low-confidence valuation for low-fiber or low-whole-grain intake where a sustained +8 g/day fiber-equivalent change is realistic and GI tolerance is acceptable. Requires baseline intake, food access, diet-quality context, and whether the change is part of a broader diet bundle before recommendation.

Applies narrowly to a confirmed pathogenic or likely pathogenic BRCA1/BRCA2 carrier with breast tissue present who is considering bilateral risk-reducing mastectomy after genetics and surgical counseling. Do not use for VUS-only findings, unconfirmed family history, prior bilateral mastectomy, active breast cancer treatment, or patients who would not consider surgery.

Applies narrowly to a confirmed Lynch syndrome mismatch-repair pathogenic or likely pathogenic variant carrier with uterus and/or ovaries present who is considering risk-reducing hysterectomy with bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy or a staged gynecologic risk-reduction plan after genetics and gynecologic oncology counseling. Do not use for VUS-only findings, completed relevant surgery, active gynecologic cancer treatment, unresolved fertility plans, or patients who would not consider surgery.

Narrow Batch 020 valuation for advancing, progressive, or high-risk CKD where early nephrology referral and multidisciplinary care are still actionable compared with late referral or unmanaged primary-care-only follow-up. Requires eGFR, slope, albuminuria, CKD cause, current management quality, existing nephrology access, transplant/dialysis planning relevance, and local access constraints before recommendation.

Narrow Batch 020 valuation for confirmed severe symptomatic aortic stenosis where valve replacement is clinically indicated and the counterfactual is no valve replacement or delayed/inaccessible valve intervention. Requires valve metrics, symptom status, EF and flow-gradient phenotype, frailty, STS/procedural risk, TAVR/SAVR candidacy, anatomy, life expectancy, patient goals, and competing noncardiac mortality before recommendation.

Applies narrowly to confirmed impaired glucose tolerance or DPP-like high-risk prediabetes where acarbose is being considered as an incremental medication option after lifestyle and modern cardiometabolic risk management have been reviewed. Do not use for routine low-risk prediabetes, diabetes already requiring other therapy, or patients unlikely to tolerate meal-timed dosing or GI adverse effects.

Established ASCVD secondary prevention antiplatelet candidate: prior MI, ischemic stroke/TIA, coronary revascularization, chronic coronary disease, symptomatic PAD, or documented ASCVD; exclude active major bleeding, aspirin allergy, concurrent anticoagulation unless specifically modeled, and active DAPT/other antithrombotic bundles that supersede aspirin monotherapy.

Use only for confirmed serial eGFR slope decline >5 mL/min/1.73m2/year, not a single low eGFR. Best fit is DAPA-CKD-like albuminuric CKD (eGFR 25-75 mL/min/1.73m2, UACR 200-5000 mg/g) on ACEi/ARB unless intolerant; exclude type 1 diabetes and contraindications.

ai_candidate_funnel.title: Lp(a)-driven CVD risk may be underweighted
Iron and thyroid pattern may explain low energy

clinical_plan.title: Mara Chen clinical plan from scored engine state
clinical_plan.summary: Mara Chen has 0 required items separated from the optional action map. The recommended next-step set is limited to 3 scored items after library scoring, diagnostic scoring, genetics routing, and AI candidate admission.

non_required_next_steps.label: Supervised resistance training

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Resistance training has strong function evidence and supportive observational prevention evidence, but hard-outcome QALY magnitude is translated.

non_required_next_steps.why_now: Highest patient-value optional action after eligibility and required-item separation.

non_required_next_steps.label: Coronary calcium scoring

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Guideline-supported reclassification for uncertain statin decisions; downstream QALY is modeled from lipid/BP intensity changes.

non_required_next_steps.why_now: Plaque burden changes lipid/BP intensity, urgency, and sequencing.

non_required_next_steps.label: Baseline DEXA

evidence_axis.basis_summary: DXA bone screening is guideline-supported in eligible women/risk groups; lean-mass/body-composition interpretation is weaker and should be split later.

non_required_next_steps.why_now: Low bone density, high visceral fat, or low lean mass changes resistance training, nutrition, medication thresholds, and monitoring cadence.
